feature_name: v1.15.0 "UI polish + yt-dlp enhancements + infra" -- one big swing (9 items across UI polish, yt-dlp module, and infra)
base: main. NOTE: the working tree is currently on branch feat/icon-sets; the coordinator owns ALL git and will create feature/v1.15-bigswing off main before implementation. Prior shipped records: docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-v1.14-quickwins.md (random sort, mobile logo, hide-from-sidebar, default view), 2026-07-06-ytdlp-metube-parity.md (v1.12 one-off download endpoint + dropdowns + live status + synthetic Downloads folder), 2026-07-06-v1.13-polish.md.
git_ownership_note: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task.
node_toolchain_note: Node 22 is the toolchain standard. A Node 22 toolchain is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin -- build-verify MUST run tests on Node 22 (not just fnm's default). fnm PATH export still needed for the default: export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH".
process_plan: RIGHT-SIZED PER ITEM (Dean's ask). ONE big round of ~9 items. HEAVIER review (careful QA + adversarial finder / two-reviewer gate) reserved for the security/spawn-args + folder-model items: item 5 (nicer filenames / SF4), item 4 (skip-shorts / --match-filter spawn args), item 6 (one-off archive bypass), item 1 (folder DnD order model). LIGHTER (SDE + build-verify on Node 22 + single QA review) for the UI/infra items: item 2 (mobile scale), item 3 (one-off header button+modal), item 7 (transcode dir env + CRF), item 8 (PWA PNG, may DEFER), item 9 (core test coverage). Dean's on-device pass is the ARBITER for all visual items (1, 2, 3). Ship cadence: merge -> tag v1.15.0 -> Docker publish (CI is the image arbiter) -> Dean's on-device pass.
hard_constraints: OPTIONAL yt-dlp MODULE DISABLED-PATH NO-OP IS SACRED (items 3, 4, 5, 6). When FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED is off, FileTube stays byte-identical: no new routes, no new UI, no header button, no modal markup/assets leaked. Item 3's header download button MUST appear ONLY when the module is enabled -- gate it exactly like the /subscriptions nav-injection in public/js/common.js (fetch('/api/subscriptions/health') -> inject only on ok; fail closed on 404/network). No module JS/markup served from public/ unconditionally that reveals the feature when disabled.

PATH-CONFINEMENT / SF4 STAYS INTACT (item 5). --restrict-filenames (args.js:390) is a defense-in-depth guard, NOT to be simply dropped. The resolveChannelDir + isPathUnder/realpathUnderChannelDir/quarantineEscapedDownloads confinement (args.js:170-239, index.js quarantine) stays. Any nicer-filename template MUST preserve path-traversal confinement AND keep id-based uniqueness so --download-archive dedup + cleanDisplayTitle still work.

SUBSCRIPTION DEDUP IS UNCHANGED (item 6). The one-off archive-bypass affects the ONE-OFF path ONLY (runOneShot, index.js:694). Subscriptions still use --download-archive (args.js buildYtdlpDownloadArgs / buildYtdlpListArgs) and 'deleted stays gone' (D3) is preserved.

ARG-ARRAY / NO-SHELL SPAWN DISCIPLINE (item 4). Any --match-filter value that reaches argv is built as its own argv element via the pure builders in args.js -- never concatenated, never a shell string. Follow the shouldSkip/normalizeQuality allowlist-and-re-assert posture; a skipShorts value is validated at the store boundary (validateSubscriptionInput/updateSubscription) AND re-asserted at build time.

SYNTHETIC 'Downloads' FOLDER STAYS OUT OF db.folders (item 1). The v1.12/v1.13 synthetic download folder is display-only (extraScanRoots is the sole authority; C3/C7/E1 invariants). DnD reordering of that entry MUST persist via folderSettings[downloadDir].order (the v1.13.0 up/down path), NEVER by writing it into db.folders. Real folders reorder via db.folders positional order.

TRANSCODE-CACHE HOUSEKEEPING MUST NOT BREAK (item 7). A configurable TRANSCODE_DIR must keep the size-cap LRU eviction, age-retention sweep, and lastServedAt logic working -- they all key off the transcode dir (server.js:24, 200, 307-320, 382-400, 1722-1745). Both encode sites use hardcoded -crf 23 today: the lazy on-disk transcode (server.js:691) and the live desktop pipe (server.js:2038) -- a higher-CRF option must cover the site(s) that produce cached files; PE decides whether live-pipe CRF is in scope. Defensive path handling: mkdir -p, writability check, fall back or error clearly.

follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes; prefer textContent over innerHTML for NEW dynamic strings (XSS-safe -- especially the item-3 modal status + item-1 DnD labels); node:test for new/regression tests; lint 0 warnings; NO new runtime dependencies (item 8 PWA-PNG explicitly must NOT add a heavy image dep -- DEFER instead).
Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties / --vars). Any new CSS (items 2, 3) must use existing tokens; no hardcoded colors that break themes.

ADDITIVE / NO REGRESSIONS to the existing home sort/sidebar/setup/watch behavior, the existing Setup up/down folder reorder (item 1 augments, keeps keyboard/tap fallback for mobile), the existing /subscriptions one-off form (item 3 -- PM decides keep-or-de-emphasize), and the disabled-module byte-identical guarantee.

Item 4 gotcha: a new skipShorts field must be threaded end-to-end (store.js validateSubscriptionInput + updateSubscription + ensureYtdlp backfill undefined->false; the PATCH edit handler in lib/ytdlp/index.js; the subscriptions UI toggle). A field not added to the store validators/allowlist is silently dropped, mirroring the members-only toggle (allowMembersOnly) already in store.js.

Item 9: additive tests ONLY -- do not rewrite/relocate existing tests. Keep FFmpeg/yt-dlp binaries OUT of the automated node:test suite (not installed on CI). Target the risky/untested CORE paths (scan/metadata/transcode-eviction pure logic + HTTP smoke tests), not 100% coverage.

product_forks_for_dean.item_4_skipshorts_scope: RECOMMEND per-subscription skipShorts toggle (mirrors the existing per-sub members-only posture; a subscription record already carries format/quality/filetype/maxVideos/paused). A global default is OPTIONAL and can be deferred -- per-sub is the right granularity and lower risk. Default = download everything (opt-IN to skip). PE designs the exact yt-dlp --match-filter (duration-based, e.g. duration>60, and/or excluding the /shorts tab); PM confirms the product behavior (per-sub only vs also-global). Recommendation: per-sub only for v1.15.
product_forks_for_dean.item_5_filename_template: RECOMMEND PE explores --windows-filenames (allows spaces + more readable chars, still avoids traversal-dangerous characters) as the likely path, keeping the ` [<id>].<ext>` id suffix for uniqueness/archive-matching and keeping the post-download confinement/quarantine guard. Alternatives for PE: a custom -o template with --replace-in-metadata, or keeping --restrict-filenames but improving OUTPUT_TEMPLATE (args.js:270). NON-NEGOTIABLE: whatever is chosen keeps SF4 path confinement and does not break --download-archive dedup or cleanDisplayTitle. This is the trickiest item -- full T3/T4 security care. PM states the user-visible goal (readable on-disk names with spaces); PE owns the exact flag.
product_forks_for_dean.item_6_redownload_overwrite_vs_copy: RECOMMEND overwrite (simplest, matches user intent 'download THIS video again'). For a one-off, bypass the shared archive (--no-download-archive and/or --force-overwrites) so an already-archived URL still downloads. Alternative: let yt-dlp make a ' (1)' copy. Overwrite is the recommended default; PM/PE confirm. MUST NOT change subscription dedup. Verify the re-download still lands in the right folder + indexes.
product_forks_for_dean.item_7_transcode_crf_default: RECOMMEND the CRF default stays 23 (today's quality) and the higher-CRF (smaller-file) option is OPT-IN via env (e.g. TRANSCODE_CRF) and/or a Settings value -- do NOT silently change existing users' transcode quality. PM confirms whether it is env-only or also a Settings-UI control (env-only is lower risk for v1.15). Also confirm TRANSCODE_DIR default stays data/transcoded.
product_forks_for_dean.item_8_pwa_png_defer: RECOMMEND attempt WITHOUT a new dependency (a tiny pure-JS/SVG rasterization build step, hand-authored PNGs, or a lightweight approach). If no clean no-new-dep path exists, DEFER (keep on the roadmap / tech-debt tracker) and say so explicitly -- do NOT add a heavy image dependency just for the home-screen icon. PM notes the acceptance as 'either PNGs land with no new dep, or item is explicitly deferred with a tracked note'.
product_forks_for_dean.item_1_dnd_fallback: RECOMMEND DnD is a progressive ENHANCEMENT over the existing Setup up/down buttons and mobile tap reorder -- keep a keyboard/tap-accessible fallback (do not remove the up/down affordance on mobile unless a tap-based reorder replaces it). Vanilla DOM, no new deps. PM confirms whether the up/down buttons stay alongside DnD on desktop or are replaced by DnD there.
product_forks_for_dean.item_3_oneoff_existing_form: MINOR: the existing /subscriptions page already has the one-off download form (URL + format/quality/filetype dropdowns, reusing POST /api/ytdlp/download). Item 3 adds a HEADER button + compact MODAL (Dean-locked placement) that reuses the SAME endpoint + dropdowns + GET /api/subscriptions/status polling. PM decides: keep the /subscriptions one-off form as-is, or de-emphasize it now that the header modal is the primary surface. RECOMMEND keep it (low cost) but make the header modal the prominent path.

1.title: Folder drag-and-drop reordering (Setup list AND left sidebar)
1.current_behavior_confirmed: Folder order has TWO sources: real folders = db.folders positional order; the synthetic v1.12/v1.13 'Downloads' folder = folderSettings[downloadDir].order (NEVER in db.folders -- display-only via extraScanRoots, C3/C7/E1). Setup has up/down reorder buttons today. Sidebar render is client-side (public/js/main.js renderSidebarFolders).
1.fix: Replace/augment the up/down buttons with drag-and-drop in BOTH the Setup folder list AND the left sidebar. Reuse the existing order mechanisms: db.folders positional order for real folders; folderSettings[downloadDir].order for the synthetic Downloads folder (persist DnD of it via that path, same as v1.13 up/down). Keyboard/tap fallback for mobile (DnD is enhancement). Vanilla DOM, no new deps.
files: public/setup.html + public/js/main.js (or setup client) -- Setup folder list DnD
server.js POST /api/config -- folderSettings order + db.folders order persistence (already supports 'order')
1.arbiter: Dean on-device (DnD UX) + integration test for the synthetic-folder-order-stays-out-of-db.folders invariant

2.title: Mobile UI scale polish (v1.14.0 sort-row overflow regression + card sizing)
2.current_behavior_confirmed: The home sort-controls row (heading + 'Newest first' <select> + the v1.14.0 'Shuffle again' button) OVERFLOWS the mobile viewport -- the shuffle button is cut off on the right. Video cards read a bit large on mobile.
2.fix: (a) Make the sort row wrap/scale on mobile -- stack controls below the heading and/or shorten the shuffle button to an icon on mobile. (b) Tighten card sizing on mobile so more than ~one card is comfortably in view. Touches public/css/style.css mobile media queries + the sort-row markup in public/index.html. Use existing era-theme tokens.

3.title: One-off download: HEADER BUTTON + COMPACT MODAL (Dean-locked placement)
3.review_weight: LIGHTER (UI) -- but gated-injection discipline is load-bearing
3.current_behavior_confirmed: POST /api/ytdlp/download exists (one-off endpoint, index.js:967 route -> runOneShot index.js:694), with format/quality/filetype dropdowns already on the /subscriptions one-off form (v1.12/v1.13). GET /api/subscriptions/status returns live activity (activity.getSnapshot, includes oneShots). The nav-injection gating pattern lives in public/js/common.js: fetch('/api/subscriptions/health') -> inject only when ok, fail closed on 404/network (injectSubscriptionsNavLinkIfEnabled, common.js:601-648).
3.fix: Add a small download icon-button in the header next to Settings that opens a COMPACT MODAL (URL input + format/quality/filetype dropdowns + Download button + live status). CRITICAL: button + modal appear ONLY when the yt-dlp module is enabled -- gate via the same health-probe injection pattern as the subscriptions nav link (common.js). When disabled, the header is byte-identical (no button, no modal, no leak). Reuse POST /api/ytdlp/download + the T5 dropdown controls + GET /api/subscriptions/status polling for live status. Add to pages with the header (index.html at least; watch.html/setup.html as sensible). XSS-safe textContent for all dynamic status strings.
files: public/js/common.js (header button + modal injection, gated on /api/subscriptions/health)
reuses lib/ytdlp POST /api/ytdlp/download + GET /api/subscriptions/status (no server change expected)
3.arbiter: Dean on-device (visual/UX) + integration test that the button/modal are absent when the module is disabled

4.title: Skip YouTube Shorts toggle (per-subscription)
4.current_behavior_confirmed: Per-sub gating already follows the shouldSkip pattern (lib/ytdlp/rules.js) for members-only/availability; the members-only toggle is allowMembersOnly in store.js. Subscription records carry format/quality/filetype/maxVideos/paused, validated in store.js validateSubscriptionInput (:188) + updateSubscription (:236) with ensureYtdlp backfill. Arg builders are pure allowlist-and-re-assert (args.js).
4.fix: Add a per-subscription skipShorts toggle (recommend per-sub; global default optional -- see fork). Implement via yt-dlp --match-filter (PE designs the exact filter: duration-based e.g. duration>60, and/or exclude the /shorts path). Follow the rules.js shouldSkip / allowlist posture; validate any value reaching argv (arg-array/no-shell). Default = download everything (opt-in to skip). New validated skipShorts field on the sub record (backfill undefined->false, non-destructive) + the PATCH edit handler + the UI toggle.
files: lib/ytdlp/index.js (PATCH /api/subscriptions/:id wiring; match-filter applied in the list/download pass)
lib/ytdlp/rules.js (if a pure shouldSkipShort helper is warranted)
4.arbiter: unit tests (arg builder + validation) + integration (PATCH round-trip) + on-device confirmation Shorts are excluded

5.title: Nicer on-disk filenames (keep SF4 confinement)
5.current_behavior_confirmed: Downloads are named via --restrict-filenames (args.js:390, ASCII-folded/underscored) + OUTPUT_TEMPLATE '%(title)s [%(id)s].%(ext)s' (args.js:270), e.g. Link_Miguel_..._en_Vivo [wN4p6TKlBzQ].mp3 -- odd on disk. UI display is already clean via cleanDisplayTitle. SF4 confinement: resolveChannelDir + isPathUnder/realpathUnderChannelDir + post-download quarantine (args.js:170-239, index.js quarantineEscapedDownloads).
5.fix: Improve on-disk names to be more readable WITHOUT losing (a) path-confinement safety (--restrict-filenames is an SF4 defense-in-depth guard; do NOT simply drop it) and (b) id-based uniqueness / archive matching. PE explores --windows-filenames (spaces, still safe) vs a custom -o template vs --replace-in-metadata vs keeping restrict + improving the template. MUST preserve the resolveChannelDir/startsWith(root+sep) confinement AND the download-archive dedup AND cleanDisplayTitle. T3/T4 security care.
files: possibly server.js cleanDisplayTitle if the on-disk shape changes (verify it still cleans correctly)
5.arbiter: unit tests (arg-array shape + confinement helpers) + adversarial review + on-device confirmation names are readable and safe

6.title: One-off re-download of an already-downloaded URL (archive bypass for one-offs only)
6.current_behavior_confirmed: One-off download (runOneShot, index.js:694) calls run.runDownload with the SAME --download-archive dedup as subscriptions (args.js buildYtdlpDownloadArgs). So a one-off of a URL already in the archive is currently SKIPPED. Subscriptions rely on the archive (D3 'deleted stays gone').
6.fix: For a ONE-OFF only, bypass the shared archive (e.g. --no-download-archive and/or --force-overwrites) so an explicitly-requested video downloads even if already present. MUST NOT change subscription dedup (subscriptions still use the archive). Verify the one-off still lands in the right folder + indexes; overwrite-vs-'(1)'-copy per the fork (recommend overwrite). PE designs whether the archive flag is dropped, a separate arg-builder branch is added for one-offs, or a per-call opt.
files: lib/ytdlp/args.js (a one-off download-args path/branch, or an opt to omit --download-archive / add --force-overwrites)
test/unit ytdlp args tests (subscription args UNCHANGED; one-off args omit archive)
6.arbiter: unit tests (subscription args retain archive; one-off args bypass it) + on-device re-download confirmation

7.title: Configurable transcode dir (env) + higher-CRF option
7.current_behavior_confirmed: TRANSCODE_DIR = path.join(DATA_DIR, 'transcoded') (server.js:24), mkdir on boot (:33). Size-cap LRU eviction + age-retention sweep + lastServedAt all key off TRANSCODE_DIR (server.js:200, 307-320, 382-400, 1722-1745). CRF is hardcoded '23' at TWO encode sites: the lazy on-disk transcode (server.js:691) and the live desktop pipe (server.js:2038).
7.fix: (a) A TRANSCODE_DIR env var so the cache can live on roomy NFS instead of local disk (default = today's data/transcoded). Defensive: mkdir -p, writability check. Must NOT break the size-cap eviction / age-retention / lastServedAt logic (they key off the dir). (b) A higher-CRF option (env e.g. TRANSCODE_CRF and/or a setting) for smaller files -- default stays 23. PE/PM confirm whether CRF applies to the live-pipe site too (recommend the cached on-disk site at minimum).
files: server.js (TRANSCODE_DIR from env with default + writability guard; TRANSCODE_CRF constant applied at the encode site(s) :691 and possibly :2038)
test/unit (transcode dir/CRF config parsing, mirroring parseCacheCap)
7.arbiter: unit tests (env parsing + eviction still keyed off dir) + build-verify + on-device with a custom dir

8.title: PWA home-screen icon PNG (best-effort -- may DEFER)
8.current_behavior_confirmed: The web manifest is wired but needs raster PNGs from the SVG. Previously parked ('no rasterizer handy').
8.fix: Attempt WITHOUT adding a heavy dep -- a tiny build step, hand-authored PNGs, or a pure-JS/SVG approach. If no clean no-new-dep path exists, DEFER (keep on roadmap / tech-debt tracker) and say so explicitly. Do NOT add a big image dependency just for this.
8.arbiter: either PNGs land with no new dep (Dean on-device home-screen add), or explicit tracked deferral

9.title: Broaden core test coverage (additive)
9.current_behavior_confirmed: The yt-dlp module is well-tested; the CORE app (scan/metadata/transcode-eviction logic + HTTP endpoints) has thinner coverage.
9.fix: Backfill the highest-value gaps: unit tests for scan/metadata/transcode-eviction pure logic + HTTP smoke tests for the main endpoints. Scope sensibly (target risky/untested core paths, not 100%). Additive tests only; do not rewrite existing tests. Keep FFmpeg/yt-dlp out of the automated suite.
files: test/unit/ (scan/metadata/eviction pure helpers exported from server.js)
test/integration/ (HTTP smoke against a temp DATA_DIR)
9.arbiter: build-verify green on Node 22 + coverage of the newly-tested core paths

pe_design_items: Items needing PE design (route to Design after Discovery): 4 (skip-shorts --match-filter), 5 (nicer-filename template preserving SF4), 6 (one-off archive-bypass branch), 1 (folder DnD order model incl. synthetic-folder order), 3 (header button + modal gated injection). Items 2, 7, 8, 9 are more direct (UI/infra/tests) -- PE gives a light design pass or the EM can task them directly, but they still ride through Design for a cohesive single exec plan.
cross_cutting: Items interact: item 3 (header modal) + item 6 (one-off re-download) + item 5 (nicer filenames) all flow through the SAME one-off path (POST /api/ytdlp/download -> runOneShot -> run.runDownload -> args.buildYtdlpDownloadArgs), so their changes must compose (a nicer-filename one-off that bypasses the archive still confines + indexes). Item 4 (skip-shorts) + item 5 (filenames) both edit args.js pure builders -- keep the arg-array/no-shell discipline uniform. Item 1 (folder DnD) + the synthetic Downloads folder order (item from v1.12/v1.13) share folderSettings[downloadDir].order. Item 7 (transcode dir/CRF) + item 9 (tests) -- item 9 should add coverage for the new transcode config. NO new deps; disabled-module byte-identical guarantee holds across items 3/4/5/6.

history.note: New feature round v1.15.0 'UI polish + yt-dlp enhancements + infra' initiated by Dean as ONE big swing (~9 items), target branch feature/v1.15-bigswing off main (coordinator-owned git; working tree currently on feat/icon-sets). UI POLISH: (1) folder drag-and-drop reordering in Setup + sidebar, (2) mobile UI scale polish (v1.14 sort-row overflow + card sizing). YT-DLP: (3) one-off download header button + compact modal (Dean-locked placement, gated on module-enabled), (4) skip-Shorts per-sub toggle via --match-filter, (5) nicer on-disk filenames keeping SF4 --restrict-filenames confinement, (6) one-off re-download of already-archived URL (archive bypass for one-offs only). INFRA: (7) configurable TRANSCODE_DIR env + higher-CRF option, (8) PWA home-screen PNG (best-effort, may DEFER, no new dep), (9) broaden core test coverage (additive). RIGHT-SIZED process: HEAVIER review (careful QA + adversarial finder / two-reviewer) for the security/spawn-args + folder-model items 5,4,6,1; LIGHTER (SDE + build-verify Node 22 + single QA) for UI/infra items 2,3,7,8,9. Dean on-device pass = arbiter for visual items 1,2,3. Ship: merge -> tag v1.15.0 -> Docker publish -> Dean's on-device pass. Reset state from the completed v1.14 record to this v1.15 feature.

EM VERIFIED current code against all 9 items before routing: (3) POST /api/ytdlp/download one-off endpoint (index.js:967->runOneShot:694), format/quality/filetype dropdowns + GET /api/subscriptions/status live status already shipped (v1.12/v1.13); the gated-injection pattern to copy is injectSubscriptionsNavLinkIfEnabled in public/js/common.js:601-648 (fetch /api/subscriptions/health -> inject only on ok, fail closed). (4) per-sub gating follows rules.js shouldSkip; sub records validated in store.js validateSubscriptionInput:188 + updateSubscription:236 + ensureYtdlp backfill (members-only = allowMembersOnly) -> skipShorts must thread there + the PATCH handler + UI. (5) filenames = --restrict-filenames (args.js:390) + OUTPUT_TEMPLATE '%(title)s [%(id)s].%(ext)s' (args.js:270); SF4 confinement = resolveChannelDir + isPathUnder/realpathUnderChannelDir + quarantine (args.js:170-239). (6) runOneShot (index.js:694) uses the SAME --download-archive dedup as subs -> bypass for one-offs only. (7) TRANSCODE_DIR = path.join(DATA_DIR,'transcoded') (server.js:24, mkdir :33), eviction/age/lastServedAt key off it (:200,:307-320,:382-400,:1722-1745); CRF hardcoded '23' at TWO sites (lazy on-disk :691, live pipe :2038). Wrote the Discovery inbox for product-manager at .state/inbox/product-manager.md (turn the 9 into grouped, tagged ACs; note which need PE design: 4,5,6,1,3). Surfaced 6 forks for Dean with recommendations: skip-shorts per-sub only (rec), nicer-filename via --windows-filenames (PE, rec), one-off re-download overwrite (rec), transcode CRF default 23 + opt-in higher (rec), PWA-PNG attempt-no-dep-else-defer (rec), DnD progressive-enhancement-keep-fallback (rec). Set artifacts.requirements/exec_plan to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.15-bigswing.md (PM to author). Routing to PM; then /prep-pe-design.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery. Authored docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.15-bigswing.md: Goal/Scope/Out-of-scope/Constraints, FR-1..FR-9 (one per scope item), and 52 grouped+tagged acceptance criteria (45 item-scoped across items 1-9 + 7 cross-cutting), tagged [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]/[MANUAL]/[PROCESS]. All 6 forks from product_forks_for_dean restated as resolved (not re-opened; no new fork surfaced): item 4 skip-shorts = per-sub only; item 5 filenames = --windows-filenames-style direction, PE owns exact template, SF4/archive/cleanDisplayTitle preserved; item 6 re-download = overwrite, one-off-only bypass; item 7 CRF = default 23 unchanged, higher-CRF opt-in via env, TRANSCODE_DIR default data/transcoded; item 8 PWA PNG = attempt no-dep else defer with tracked note; item 1 DnD = progressive enhancement, fallback stays, synthetic-folder order via folderSettings[downloadDir].order only; item 3 = keep the existing /subscriptions one-off form alongside the new header modal. Verified no active exec-plan overlap (docs/exec-plans/active/ was empty pre-this-plan) and no out-of-scope item conflicts with docs/CONTRIBUTING.md (item 9 explicitly keeps tests in scope). Cross-cutting ACs cover the disabled-module byte-identical guarantee (items 3/4/5/6), arg-array/no-shell discipline, cookie-redaction continuity, Node 22, the two-reviewer gate for items 1/4/5/6 vs single-QA for 2/3/7/8/9, no-new-deps, and lint 0. Design items flagged for principal-engineer: 4, 5, 6, 1, 3 (mechanics not chosen); items 2, 7, 8, 9 are more direct but still ride through Design for one cohesive plan. Advanced stage discovery -> design; artifacts.requirements/artifacts.exec_plan unchanged (already pointing at the plan). Ready for Design stage (principal-engineer).
